pullRequest.title: Equality of PauliErrors
pullRequest.body:
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Context:** `qml.equal` returned an error when called with `PauliError` instances since there was no specific type dispatch for `PauliErrors` and the default `Operator` case was called which compares `op1.data` and `op2.data` with `np.allclose` in `equal.py`. In the case of `PauliError`, `op.data` includes a Pauli string which is a `str` and therefore `np.allclose` raises. [sc-68793]

**Description of the Change:** Makes `operators` a hyperparameter rather than data.

**Benefits:** Allows the equality of `PauliErrors` to be checked.

**Possible Drawbacks:** 

**Related GitHub Issues:**

Fixes https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/pennylane/issues/5998

pullRequest.title: Fix `qml.TShift` power method
pullRequest.body:
This PR implements a fix for issue #6355 (`pow` method for non parametric operators acting on a single qutrit).

The method `Operator.pow` is supposed to return a list with a simplified "decomposition" of the given operator. For example, in the case of `TShift` we have `TShift**3` = `Identity` and so `TShift.pow(3)` returns an empty list. This PR handles the general case `TShift.pow(z)` returning a list with `z%3` copies of the given operator (actually the only relevant case is when `z=2`).

This fix should work also for the `qml.TClock` and `qml.TAdd` operators.

[sc-75304]

pullRequest.title: Enable `cancel_inverses` to cancel nested pairs of mutually inverse gates
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**
`cancel_inverses` cancels one pair of gates at a time and then moves on through the circuit. This means that nested pairs of mutually inverse gates are not cancelled.

In addition, `cancel_inverses` as an almost-bug undesired behaviour #6729.

**Description of the Change:**
Add keyword argument `recursive` that enables iterative cancellation of such nested pairs. It is enabled by default and
can be disabled for backwards-compatible behaviour.

Note that repeatedly applying `cancel_inverses` can lead to the same result, but it is not known a priori how many times it needs to be called, and calling it repeatedly is less efficient.

In addition, this PR alters behaviour slightly to fix #6729.

to do:
- [x] discuss naming of kwarg.
- [x] ~~plxpr transform~~.

**Benefits:**
Better compilation pass quality.
"bug" fix

**Possible Drawbacks:**
N/A

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Fix #6729 
[sc-80821]

pullRequest.title: Fix `PhaseShift` operation with a batch size of 1
pullRequest.body:
The `PhaseShift` operation was raising an unexpected error when used with a batch size of 1 on the `default.qubit` device (see #6874 and sc-82871). This PR fixes the issue to return the expected output, for example:
```python
import pennylane as qml
import numpy as np

@qml.qnode(qml.device("default.qubit"))
def circuit(x):
    qml.PhaseShift(x, wires=0)
    return qml.expval(qml.Z(0))

params = np.array([1.57])
print(circuit(params))
```
`[1.]`

Fixes https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/pennylane/issues/6874

pullRequest.title: fix: allow autograph to work on wrapped `adjoint` and `ctrl` functions
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

```python
import pennylane as qml

qml.capture.enable()

dev = qml.device("default.qubit", wires=5)

def adjoint_fn(y):
    if y < 2:
        qml.RX(y, 0)
        qml.RY(y, 0)
    else:
        qml.RY(y, 0)
        qml.RZ(y, 0)

    qml.Hadamard(0)

@qml.qnode(dev)
def f(x):
    qml.adjoint(adjoint_fn)(x)
    return qml.expval(qml.Z(0))

qml.capture.run_autograph(f)(1.5)
```
previously failed. Now it correctly returns a result.

**Description of the Change:**

Changes which function gets selected for the convert call patch. This follows what Catalyst does.

**Benefits:** Mimic Catalyst.

**Possible Drawbacks:** None identified.

**Related GitHub Issues:** Fixes #6992 

[sc-84934]

pullRequest.title: [Capture] Fix plxpr transforms that don't take dynamic wires into account
pullRequest.body:
[sc-90121]

**Context:**
Fixes #7349.

**Description of the Change:**
* Update plxpr `cancel_inverses` to interpret all previous ops if an op with dynamic wires is encountered.
* Update plxpr `merge_rotations` to interpret all previous ops if an op with dynamic wires is encountered.
* Update plxpr `single_qubit_fusion` to interpret all previous ops if an op with dynamic wires is encountered.
* Update plxpr `merge_amplitude_embedding` to merge all `AmplitudeEmbedding`s found so far and all ops encountered so far if an op with dynamic wires is encountered.
  * If an `AmplitudeEmbedding` is found after a non-`AmplitudeEmbedding` op _with dynamic wires_, an error is raised.
  * If an `AmplitudeEmbedding` _with dynamic wires_ is found after any non-`AmplitudeEmbedding` op, an error is raised.
  * If the above conditions _are not_ met, but there are multiple `AmplitudeEmbedding`s, no error is raised regardless of presence of dynamic wires.
* Update program capture sharp bits page to notify users that non-native transforms may have incorrect behaviour with dynamic wires. 

**Benefits:**
* Correct behaviour of above transforms with program capture
* Better information to users about sharp bits

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**
#7349

pullRequest.title: Fix `qml.FlipSign` operation
pullRequest.body:
Fix #7350.

The original example in the linked issue should actually raise an error because `qml.FlipSign(2, wires=2)` is trying to flip the sign of the basis state `2` (or `[1,0]` in binary repr) targeting a single wire.

Taking a deeper look into this, I also noticed that `qml.FlipSign` supports targeting a sub system of the circuit as well. For instance, with `n=[0]` and `wires=[0]` in a circuit with 2 wires, it flips the sign of all the basis states where the first (left-most) qubit is 0 (i.e. 00 and 01).
```python
import pennylane as qml

num_wires = 2
dev = qml.device("default.qubit", wires=num_wires)

@qml.qnode(dev)
def circuit():
    for wire in range(num_wires):
        qml.Hadamard(wires=wire)
    qml.FlipSign(n=[0], wires=[0])
    return qml.state()
```
```
>>> circuit()
array([-0.5+0.j, -0.5+0.j,  0.5+0.j,  0.5+0.j])
```
However, this is currently not documented nor tested at all. If that is the expected behaviour, I think it would make sense to include docs and tests as part of this PR. Any feedback is welcome!

[sc-90122]

pullRequest.title: Fix trainability of classical preprocessing in transform programs
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**
`validate_device_wires` does not preserve the `trainable_params` property of the tape if there are any measurements that do not have `obs` or `wires`, like `qml.probs()`. 

**Previous context:**
For differentiation-related transforms, the classical Jacobian needs to be obtained during program transform execution.
For this, the classical preprocessing is traced throughout the transform program, and the _trainable_ parameters of the processed tape, as indicated by `trainable_params`, are returned from `_classical_preprocessing`. This function can then be differentiated to obtain the classical Jacobian.

Currently, the trainability of the tape created from a QNode is maintained correctly. However, applying a transform program is not guaranteed to set the correct `trainable_params` on the transformed tape. This makes `_classical_preprocessing` return _all_ parameters, instead of just those that should be trainable based on QNode-level information. Consequently, the classical Jacobian has wrong dimensions.

**Description of the Change:**
Pass the `trainable_params` of the original tape to the new, modified tape in `validate_device_wires`, if there is modification at all.

**In addition: ** Update the `trainable_params` of the tape transformed via the transform program, so that `_classical_preprocessing` returns trainable parameters only, even if some intermediate transform did not maintain the information.

**Benefits:**
Bug fix for differentiation-related transforms with classical cotransforms applied to QNodes.

**Possible Drawbacks:**
N/A

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Fixes #7356 
[sc-90203]

pullRequest.title: Improve drawing of `GlobalPhase` and controlled `GlobalPhase`
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

`GlobalPhase` is drawn without grouping symbols if given no wires, but with grouping symbols if it is given wires. 

`GlobalPhase`, when initialized with non-empty wires, is drawn only on the initialized wires.

controlled `GlobalPhase` is drawn very confusingly, see #7456 

`GlobalPhase` operations do not get their own layer in MPLdrawer

controlled `GlobalPhase` can not be drawn in MPLdrawer.

**Description of the Change:**
Draw grouping symbols for `GlobalPhase` and controlled `GlobalPhase`. Stop drawing the "GlobalPhase" label on control nodes of controlled `GlobalPhase`.

Example:

```python
import pennylane as qml

def f():
    qml.ctrl(qml.GlobalPhase(0.5), (1,2))
    qml.GlobalPhase(0.5, wires=[0, 3])
    qml.GlobalPhase(0.5)

print(qml.draw(f, wire_order=[0,1,2,3], show_all_wires=True)())
```

Before:
```pycon
0: ──GlobalPhase(0.50)──╭GlobalPhase(0.50)──GlobalPhase(0.50)─┤
1: ─╭●GlobalPhase(0.50)─│───────────────────GlobalPhase(0.50)─┤
2: ─╰●GlobalPhase(0.50)─│───────────────────GlobalPhase(0.50)─┤  
3: ──GlobalPhase(0.50)──╰GlobalPhase(0.50)──GlobalPhase(0.50)─┤
```
After:
```pycon
0: ─╭GlobalPhase(0.50)─╭GlobalPhase(0.50)─╭GlobalPhase(0.50)─┤  
1: ─├●─────────────────├GlobalPhase(0.50)─├GlobalPhase(0.50)─┤  
2: ─├●─────────────────├GlobalPhase(0.50)─├GlobalPhase(0.50)─┤  
3: ─╰GlobalPhase(0.50)─╰GlobalPhase(0.50)─╰GlobalPhase(0.50)─┤  
```

**Benefits:**
Better drawing

**Possible Drawbacks:**
None identified

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Closes #7456 
[sc-91554]

pullRequest.title: Fix `map_to_standard_wires` to work with work wires
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**
`qml.tape.QuantumScript.map_to_standard_wires` maps the circuit wires to some standard choice of wires, with the following conventions:
- The new wires are consecutive integers starting at 0.
- Wires used in operations come first, including those that _also_ are acted on by measurements.
- Wires _exclusively_ acted on by measurements come after operations' wires.

If the wire order of the circuit already is compatible with these conventions, no mapping is performed.

Work wires, however, are not considered at all in this method.
As work wires of some important operations (like `MultiControlledX`) are not part of the operation's wires, they are disregarded entirely by `map_to_standard_wires`.

**Description of the Change:**
Take `work_wires` into account, extending the above conventions by the following point:
- wires _exclusively_ used as work wires come after exclusive measurement wires.

Also, this PR adds test for the method, which we did not have as unit tests.

**Benefits:**
- Fixes #7580 (note that this includes circuit execution as soon as an exclusive work wire is flying around in the circuit).
- If no decomposition involving the work wires is performed, the exclusive work wires are trailing wires that can be "ignored" away easily, i.e. there are no mysterious gaps in the standard wire ordering just because there _would_ be work wires _if_ we did the decomposition.

**Possible Drawbacks:**
I first thought that this introduces some non-canonical choices of how to handle work wires as part of operators or not. 
But this is actually not the case, because `map_to_standard_wires` basically just creates a `wire_map` that is used for mapping in `qml.map_wires`. So the choices for how to treat work wires, for example when an operation is not actually decomposed, are not affected, because they are made by `map_wires`.
This PR only changes _where_ the `work_wires` go if they are mapped by some other method (like `qml.map_wires`).

**Related GitHub Issues:**
#7580
[sc-92378]

pullRequest.title: Caching defaults to auto with execute
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

Caching can get expensive when we don't actually have duplicate tapes, so it's not worth it when `max_diff=1` the vast majority of the time. Also, it can cause confusion when it eliminates the expected randomness.

**Description of the Change:**

Defaults `cache="auto"` in `qml.execute` matching the behavior of `QNode`.

*Always* warn about caching with finite shots.

**Benefits:**

Increased performance and reduced confusion.

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**

Fixes #7643  [sc-92964]

pullRequest.title: Fix `qml.draw_mpl` with `work_wires` present
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**
`qml.draw_mpl` maps wires before drawing. 
It does not consider work wires, so that there can arise collisions between mapped algorithmic wires and non-mapped work wires. This is similar to the problem in #7580 (fixed in #7581).

**Description of the Change:**
Consider work wires in the wire mapping.
As the map is also used to decide which wires are drawn at all, we need to now produce two maps: One for mapping (now incl. work wires) and one for indicating which wires are drawn (excl. work wires).

**Benefits:**
fixed bug.

**Possible Drawbacks:**
n/a

**Related GitHub Issues:**
fixes #7659 .

[sc-93132]

pullRequest.title: Renaming PennyLane in `pyproject.toml`
pullRequest.body:
As explained in [this issue](https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/pennylane/issues/7660), the project name for PennyLane does not match with the expected binary distribution format naming conventions.

Therefore, we change the name in the `pyproject.toml` file before the next release, and this PR does precisely this.

**Related issues**: Fixes #7660 

[sc-93375]

pullRequest.title: Fix `to_zx` transform to support Toffoli and CCZ gates
pullRequest.body:
At the moment, not all the gates available in PennyLane are properly converted into their ZX-diagram representation by the `to_zx` transform. This is the reason behind issues like #7669.

This PR implements a fix to extend the support to Toffoli and CCZ gates, providing an explicit decomposition of these operations to the basis gates set used for the conversion PL circuit --> PyZX graph.

[sc-93185]

pullRequest.title: Fix `Prod.simplify()`

pullRequest.title: Metric tensor trotter fix
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

When `TrotterProduct` was in the circuit, `metric_tensor` errored out:

```
import pennylane as qml
from pennylane import numpy as pnp
from pennylane.tape import QuantumScript

# Parameter values
theta = pnp.array([pnp.pi / 4], requires_grad=True)

coeffs = [1, 1]
op = [qml.X(0), qml.X(1)]
H_Z = qml.Hamiltonian(coeffs, op)

ops = [qml.TrotterProduct(H_Z, theta, order=1, n=2)] # mt_fn works with n=1 but not with n=2

qscript = QuantumScript(ops, [qml.expval(qml.Z(0))])

mt_fn = qml.metric_tensor(qscript) # mt_fn works with n=1 but not with n=2 in TrotterProduct
```
```
ValueError: Cannot calculate ancestors for an operator that occurs multiple times.
```

**Description of the Change:**

Updates `parametrized_layers` to calculate ancestors and descendants based on the operator index, rather than trying to find the operator's position in the circuit.

**Benefits:**

Allows for the same operator instance to exist multiple times.

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**

Fixes #7717  [sc-93784]

pullRequest.title: Fix GPU selection issue in the Torch Interface
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**
The issue isn't related to the interface impl; limiting the fix to the `qml.math` single and multi dispatchers.

**Benefits:**

1. Fix the GPU selection issue in the `qml.math` wrapper function to coerce a list of (torch) tensors to all have the same dtype in `math/single_dispatch.py`

``` python 
import pennylane as qml
import torch

def circuit_builder(n):
    dev = qml.device('default.qubit', wires=n)

    def circuit(params):
        for i in range(n//2):
            qml.CRX(params[i], wires = [2*i, 2*i+1])
        return qml.state()
    
    return qml.QNode(circuit, dev, interface='torch')

print(f'Available GPUs: {torch.cuda.device_count()}')

ckt = circuit_builder(n = 2)

params0 = torch.randn(2, device='cuda:0')
print(f'params0.device: {params0.device}')
state0 = ckt(params0)
print(f'state0.device: {state0.device}')

params1 = torch.randn(2, device='cuda:1')
print(f'params1.device: {params1.device}')
state1 = ckt(params1)
print(f'state1.device: {state1.device}')
```

Installing PL from `master`: 
```
Available GPUs: 5
params0.device: cuda:0
state0.device: cuda:0
params1.device: cuda:1
state1.device: cuda:0
```

Installing PL with the patch:
```
Available GPUs: 5
params0.device: cuda:0
state0.device: cuda:0
params1.device: cuda:1
state1.device: cuda:1
```

2. Also fix the issue in `qml.math.concatenate`

``` python
import pennylane as qml
import numpy as np

import torch

print(f'Available GPUs: {torch.cuda.device_count()}')

x = torch.tensor([1, 2, 3], device='cuda:2')
y = torch.tensor([4, 5], device='cuda:2')
z = np.array([6, 7, 8])

print(qml.math.concatenate([x, y, z], axis=0))
```

Installing PL from `master`: 
```
Available GPUs: 5
tensor([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8], device='cuda:0')
```

Installing PL with the patch:
```
Available GPUs: 5
tensor([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8], device='cuda:2')
```

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Fix #7955
[sc-96197]

pullRequest.title: fix tree-traversal with non-standard wire ordering
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

```
@qml.qnode(qml.device('default.qubit'))
def c():
    qml.H(0)
    qml.CNOT((0,2))
    qml.measure(0, reset=True)
    qml.CNOT((0,1))
    return qml.expval(qml.Z(2))

c.update(mcm_method="tree-traversal")(), c.update(mcm_method="deferred")(), qml.set_shots(c, 1000).update(mcm_method="one-shot")()
```
```
(1.0, 0.0, -0.002)
```

tree-traversal is giving incorrect answers with non-sequential wire orders.

**Description of the Change:**

Add a sorted to the wire order used in the `StatePrep`.

**Benefits:**

Correct results.

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**

Fixes #7990 [sc-96701]

pullRequest.title: fix recursion error from power simplification
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

The following code caused recursion errors:
```
H = 0.25 * qml.Z(5) + 0.25* qml.Z(6) + 0.5 * qml.X(5) @ qml.X(6)

dev = qml.device("default.qubit", wires = range(7))
walk_powers_1 = [qml.pow(qml.Qubitization(H, control = [3,4]), i) for i in range(0,3)]
walk_powers_2 = [qml.pow(qml.adjoint(qml.Qubitization(H, control = [3,4])), i) for i in reversed(range(1,3))]
lcu_ops = walk_powers_2 + walk_powers_1
bessel_1 = [0.7651976865579666, 0.44005058574493355, 0.1149034849319005]
all_coeffs = [(-1)**(i+1)*bessel_1[-i] for i in range(1,3)] + bessel_1
lcu_coeffs = np.array([all_coeffs[i]*1j**(i-2) for i in range(len(all_coeffs))])
lcu = qml.dot(lcu_coeffs, lcu_ops)

@qml.qnode(dev)
def prep_sel_prep_walk():
    """
    Quantum circuit that applies PrepSelPrep to the Walk operator
    to generate the Jacobi Anger expansion to order 2.
    Returns:
    - (np.ndarray): The quantum state 
    """

    qml.PrepSelPrep(lcu, control = [0,1,2])

    return qml.state()

encoded_matrix = qml.matrix(prep_sel_prep_walk, wire_order=range(7))()[:4, :4]
print("The encoded matrix is: \n", encoded_matrix)
```

With a minimal non-working example of:
```
class DummyOp(qml.operation.Operator):
    pass

simplified = (DummyOp(0) ** 2).simplify()
```

The pow was simplifying to a `prod`, and then the `prod` was simplifying to a power.

**Description of the Change:**

Simplify the entrants to `prod` instead of the `prod` itself.

**Benefits:**

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**

Fixes #8045 [sc-97284]

pullRequest.title: Standardize the output format of `qml.specs`
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**
`qml.specs` makes very few guarantees on the shape of its output. This PR aims to make a single unified out type for `qml.specs`, which contains all of (and only) the information needed for circuit inspection.

**Description of the Change:**
* Introduces new `SpecsResources` and `CircuitSpecs` classes
* Refactors various `specs` functions throughout PennyLane to make use of the new classes
* Updates `_count_resources` to use the Catalyst-style resource names for multi-controlled gates. e.g. a 10-controlled `IsingXX` is now shown as `10C(IsingXX)` and no longer just `C(IsingXX)`. This matches how resources are counted in Catalyst.

**Benefits:**
Cleaner and more consistent `specs` returns.

Happens to also fix #8048 

**Possible Drawbacks:**
Introduces another "resources" object into PennyLane. A followup PR should restructure these PRs such that all of them inherit from a common base class.

Removes some information which used to be included in `qml.specs`, such as `num_observables` (now migrated into the resources' `measurements`) or various gradient information.

**Related GitHub Issues:**
[sc-104965] [sc-97345]
#8048

pullRequest.title: Snapshots with tree-traversal and one-shot
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

So far we've been ignoring snapshots with `one-shot` and `tree-travsesal`, as each "snapshot" can correspond to many different states.

**Description of the Change:**

The recorded measurement/ state can now correspond to a list of multiple measurements, one for each time that snapshot is encountered.

**Benefits:**

**Possible Drawbacks:**

This could balloon in size for many shots or many mcm's.  But the user would just need to accept that.

**Related GitHub Issues:**

Fixes #8125  [sc-98188]

**Description of the Change:**

Stop queuing altogether from within the mentioned methods.

**Benefits:**

**Possible Drawbacks:**
Stopping the queuing system for each call to `.operation` might be a performance regression (because in many cases there was no queuing anyways, so now we just do extra work of toggling the recording status, I suppose).

pullRequest.title: fix: update pauli arithmetic to handle abstract coefficients when jitted
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

```python
def f(c1, c2):
    op = c1 * qml.X(0) + c2 * qml.X(0)
    op.simplify()

jax.jit(f)(1.0, 1.0)
```
would fail due to pauli arithmetic not being able to handle these abstract coefficients.

**Description of the Change:**

Handle the possibility of abstract coefficients.

**Benefits:**

After testing in Catalyst, https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/catalyst/pull/2023, seems like it should fix this bug and latest/latest/latest.

**Possible Drawbacks:** None identified.

Fixes #8191

pullRequest.title: BugFix: fix incorrect depth with MCMs and conditionals
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

The current circuit graph considers the MCM and the conditional to be in the same layer as they do not have any overlapping wires, which does not take into consideration the fact that the conditional operator depends on the MCM.
```python
import pennylane as qml

@qml.qnode(qml.device("default.qubit"))
def circuit():
    m0 = qml.measure(0)
    qml.cond(m0, qml.Z)(1)
    return qml.expval(qml.Z(1))
```
```pycon
>>> qml.specs(circuit)()['resources'].depth
1
```

**Description of the Change:**

Add logic to `CircuitGraph` that connects each MCM to the conditional that depends on it.

**Benefits:**

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Fixes https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/pennylane/issues/8667
[sc-104198]

pullRequest.title: add expand transform for parameter shift
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

`param_shift_hessian` was missing an `expand_transform`. This was hidden due to fact we had been placing final transforms after device preprocessing, so unsupported gates were getting decomposed by the device.

**Description of the Change:**

Adds the same parameter shift expansion transform to `param_shift_hessian`.

**Benefits:**

Templates are properly decomposed.

**Possible Drawbacks:**

Fixes #8696  [sc-104639]

pullRequest.title: BugFix: DecompositionGraph incorrectly exclude rules
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

The decomposition graph reuses operator nodes that are not work-wire dependent, even under a different work wire budget. For example, if `CustomOp` dynamically requests a work wire to decompose to `SimpleOp`, then in graph construction, the node for `SimpleOp` will be labeled with a work wire budget of `total number of work wires - 1`. However, if the decomposition of `SimpleOp` does not use work wires, it should not be treated as "infeasible" even if the total number of work wires is 0, making the work wire budget for `SimpleOp` technically negative.

**Description of the Change:**

Adds a check to `is_feasible` and returns `True` if the decomposition does not depend on work wires.

**Benefits:**

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Fixes https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/pennylane/issues/8720
[sc-105051]

pullRequest.title: BugFix: ChangeOpBasis._flatten operator order
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

`ChangeOpBasis` internally reverses the order of operations passed in, so when `_flatten` is called, it needs to return the leaves in the reverse order so that when `_unflatten` is called, the same operator is reconstructed.

**Description of the Change:**

Additionally, the program structure of the decomposition of `TemporaryAND` is simplified.

**Benefits:**

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Fixes https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/pennylane/issues/8721
[sc-105069]

pullRequest.title: Fix resources of nested `Prod`
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**
Currently, the resource representation of `Prod` automatically flattens/unpacks inner product resource reps.
This leads to a bug whenever a decomposition is attempted to be used with the new system that contains nested products that are not unpacked themselves in the decomposition rule, for example via `lazy=False`.

**Description of the Change:**
Stop the unpacking.

**Benefits:**
Decompositions with nested products work again.

**Possible Drawbacks:**
N/A

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Fixes #8772 
[sc-105916]

pullRequest.title: Add _ipython_display_ method to qml.estimator.Resources Fix #8876
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**
Currently, when a user outputs a `qml.estimator.Resources` object in a Jupyter Notebook, it displays the raw `__repr__` output (or sometimes a generic object string), which is hard to read. The "pretty" string representation (the table view) is only accessible by explicitly calling `print()`.

**Description of the Change:**
I have implemented the `_ipython_display_` method in the `Resources` class within `pennylane/estimator/resources_base.py`. This method automatically invokes the formatted string representation when the object is returned in an IPython/Jupyter environment.
- Added `_ipython_display_` method to `Resources` class.
- Added a new test file `tests/estimator/test_resources_display.py` to verify the display logic works for both populated and empty resource objects.

**Benefits:**
Improves the User Experience (UX) in Jupyter Notebooks. Users will now see the clear, formatted summary table of resources (gates, wires, etc.) immediately upon cell execution, without needing to wrap the variable in `print()`.

**Possible Drawbacks:**
None.

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Closes #8876

pullRequest.title: Fix MemoryError in default.clifford with huge BasisState
pullRequest.body:
Fix #8946


**Context:**

Large-scale Clifford simulations (e.g., 64 wires or more than 30) were failing with a MemoryError when using `qml.BasisState`. This occurred because default.clifford attempted to generate a dense state vector for the operation, even though the state is a stabilizer state that can be efficiently represented by a Tableau.

**Description of the Change:**

I updated `pennylane/devices/default_clifford.py` to intercept `qml.BasisState` operations specifically. Instead of calling `state_vector()`, the device now iterates through the operation's decomposition and applies them directly to the underlying Stim tableau.

**Benefits:**
Can now initialize specific basis states on large numbers of qubits without raising 
``ValueError: array is too big; `arr.size * arr.dtype.itemsize` is larger than the maximum possible size.``
. (tested with 64 wires) 

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**

Related to #9015 regarding performance, but this PR focuses on fixing the crash first.

pullRequest.title: minimize number of classical registers in qasm output
pullRequest.body:
**Context:** I stumbled into the fact that even when using `measure_all=False` when exporting to QASM that classical registers still get instantiated. There are a few edge cases to worry about, but let me know if I missed any!

**Description of the Change:** Reduce the number of classical registers being created by checking which wires actually have measurement operations.

**Benefits:** This PR will make smaller QASM programs.

**Possible Drawbacks:** Slightly increased logic in the qasm module.

**Related GitHub Issues:** fixes https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/pennylane/issues/9024

**Question:** I tried to simplify the addition of measurements to the qasm string even further using the `measured_wires` variables, but I bumped into this strange fact when doing so:

```py
import pennylane as qml


dev = qml.device("default.qubit", wires=2)

tape = qml.tape.QuantumScript(
    [qml.Rot(0.3, 0.1, 0.2, wires=1)], [qml.expval(qml.PauliZ(0))]
)

print([tape.wires.index(w) for w in dev.wires])
>>> [1, 0]
```
Why does the wire ordering get reversed here?

pullRequest.body:
**Context:** Global phases were not supported in `from_qasm3` as they are not listed in the QASM 3.0 standard library at [this link](https://openqasm.com/language/standard_library.html). However, a user has requested support for it in [this issue](https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/pennylane/issues/9142).

**Description of the Change:** Adds support for `gphase` to the QASM interpreter.

**Benefits:** We can now interpret QASM including global phases.

**Possible Drawbacks:** N/A

Fixes #9142 
[sc-113272]

pullRequest.title: fix: `unitary_to_rot` recursively decomposes `QubitUnitary`s
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

The `unitary_to_rot` transform should recursively decompose any `QubitUnitary`. 

However, there exists a bug where if the `QubitUnitary` requires 0 CNOTs in its decomposition (separable prod), it will just decompose to two *single* `QubitUnitary` operations.

**Description of the Change:**

Add a recursively implementation to continuously decompose valid `QubitUnitary` until there are no more single or two-qubit ones to decompose.

**Benefits:** Transform is consistent with its name.

**Possible Drawbacks:** None identified.

Fixes #9143 
[sc-113275]

pullRequest.title: BugFix: DecompositionGraph estimates work wires incorrectly
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

The graph first tries to figure out what the minimum required number of work wires is during construction time, and then use that as a constraint when solving the graph. The issue is that when we try to figure out how many work wires is minimally required, we look at all the available decomposition rules, without considering what the gate set is, so if there exist a decomposition rule that does not use work wires, the graph thinks that this operator is solvable without work wires, even when said decomposition rule may not be reachable from the provided gate set.

**Description of the Change:**

Track whether a decomposition rule is reachable during graph construction, and only update the minimum required work wires estimate for decomposition rules that are actually reachable.

**Benefits:**

**Possible Drawbacks:**

**Related GitHub Issues:**
Fixes https://github.com/PennyLaneAI/pennylane/issues/9294
[sc-116435]

pullRequest.title: fix: prevent `debug_*` helpers from accidentally mutating the circuit
pullRequest.body:
**Context:**

Every usage of `qp.debug_state()` (to debug/inspect) mutates the circuit thus affecting the results. From a quick investigation, it seems that the device execution used to generate the data is leaking decompositions into the active queue; changing the circuit - thus changing the results.

**Description of the Change:**

1. Use `QueueingManager.stop_recording()` around the "execution" of the PLDB.
2. [BONUS] Use `QueueingManager.stop_recording()` around the logic in `_operator_decomposition_gen` as a defense in depth strategy.

**Benefits:**

Circuit is no longer mutated and consistent, correct results are obtained.

**Possible Drawbacks:** 

Two things are technically affected, but nothing that impacts any public usage.

1. User-written decomposition rules that rely on implicit outer queuing would break

This is incorrect behaviour anyways and breaks the expected contract of `compute_decomposition`. Nothing of concern.

2. Users relying on the private `_operator_decomposition_gen` function

Users should not be relying on this internal, private helper anyways. Nothing of concern.

**Related GitHub Issues:** Fixes #9343

[sc-117547]
